Dratini

#0146 Moltres #0148 Dragonair
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Dratini is a Dragon type Pokémon introduced in Generation 1.

Dratini

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0147
Type Dragon
Species Dragon Pokémon
Height 1.8 m (5′11″)
Weight 3.3 kg (7.3 lbs)
Abilities 1. Shed Skin
Marvel Scale (hidden ability)
Local № 0147 (Red/Blue/Yellow)
0241 (Gold/Silver/Crystal)
0147 (FireRed/LeafGreen)
0246 (HeartGold/SoulSilver)
0145 (X/Y — Mountain Kalos)
0281 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0379 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0147 (Let's Go Pikachu/Let's Go Eevee)
0347 (Scarlet/Violet)
0145 (Legends: Z-A)
0194 (The Crown Tundra)

Training

EV yield 1 Attack
Catch rate 45 (5.9% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 35 (lower than normal)
Base Exp. 60
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Dragon, Water 1
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 40 (10,024–10,280 steps)

Base stats

HP 41
192 286
Attack 64
119 249
Defense 45
85 207
Sp. Atk 50
94 218
Sp. Def 50
94 218
Speed 50
94 218
Total 300 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Evolution chart

#0147
Dratini
Dragon
(Level 30)
#0148
Dragonair
Dragon
(Level 55)
#0149
Dragonite
Dragon · Flying

Dratini changes

  • In Generation 1, Dratini has a base Special stat of 50.
  • In Generations 1-4, Dratini has a base experience yield of 67.
  • In Generation 2, Dratini has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Red
Blue		 Long considered a mythical POKéMON until recently when a small colony was found living underwater.
Yellow The existence of this mythical POKéMON was only recently confirmed by a fisherman who caught one.
Gold It is born large to start with. It repeatedly sheds its skin as it steadily grows longer.
Silver This POKéMON is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.
Crystal It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.
Ruby
Sapphire		 DRATINI continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.
FireRed Even the young can exceed 6.5 feet in length. It grows larger by repeatedly shedding skin.
LeafGreen Long considered a mythical POKéMON until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.
Emerald A DRATINI continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.
Diamond
Pearl
Platinum		 It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.
HeartGold It is born large to start with. It repeatedly sheds its skin as it steadily grows longer.
SoulSilver This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.
Black
White
Black 2
White 2		 It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.
X It is called the “Mirage Pokémon” because so few have seen it. Its shed skin has been found.
Y This Pokémon is full of life energy. It continually sheds its skin and grows steadily larger.
Omega Ruby
Alpha Sapphire		 Dratini continually molts and sloughs off its old skin. It does so because the life energy within its body steadily builds to reach uncontrollable levels.
Sun It grows by molting repeatedly. Boots made from the tanned cast-off skin are a super luxury item.
Moon After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.
Ultra Sun It’s still weak, so it lurks on the floor of bodies of water, eating whatever food sinks down and living a quiet life.
Ultra Moon It sheds its skin—almost on a daily basis—and grows larger. Its skin is soft just after it’s been shed.
Let's Go Pikachu
Let's Go Eevee		 Long thought to be a myth, this Pokémon’s existence was only recently confirmed by a fisherman who caught one.
Sword Dratini dwells near bodies of rapidly flowing water, such as the plunge pools of waterfalls. As it grows, Dratini will shed its skin many times.
Shield This Pokémon was long considered to be no more than a myth. The small lump on a Dratini’s forehead is actually a horn that’s still coming in.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 It is called the Mirage Pokémon because so few have seen it, but its shed skin has been found.
Scarlet It sheds many layers of skin as it grows larger. During this process, it is protected by a rapid waterfall.
Violet It is born large to start with. It repeatedly sheds its skin as it steadily grows longer.

Answers to Dratini questions

  • Can you really get Extremespeed for Dratini by Egg move?
  • Can Dratini learn Extremespeed?
  • Do you get an Extremespeed Dratini in HG/SS?
  • Is Dratini viable in LC?
  • Is it possible to pass down Extreme Speed through breeding my Dratini?
  • What happens when you fish up a Dratini in Magikarp Jump?
  • Can you get multiple Shiny Dratini/Gible from Benga?
  • In Black 2 if you have the key for the White Tree Hollow and you change it, can you get shiny dratini?
  • wheres a good place to train a lv15 dratini in HG?
  • How to boost rare spawn chances?
